improvements.title: Implement comprehensive input validation and sanitization
improvements.problem: The application currently lacks server-side validation for user inputs, making it vulnerable to XSS attacks, malicious file uploads, and abuse through unlimited API requests. User-generated content in feedback and comments is not sanitized, creating security risks. Authentication endpoints have no rate limiting, making them vulnerable to brute force attacks.
improvements.solution: Implement a multi-layered validation and security approach: (1) Add server-side validation using Zod schemas for all API route inputs, (2) Sanitize HTML content in feedback/comments using DOMPurify or similar library, (3) Add server-side file type validation using magic number detection (not just extension checking), (4) Implement file size limits on server-side (currently only client-side), (5) Add rate limiting middleware using upstash/ratelimit or express-rate-limit for all public endpoints, especially auth routes (max 5 login attempts per 15 minutes), (6) Add CAPTCHA on registration and login forms to prevent automated abuse.
userStories: As a platform operator, I want all user inputs validated so that malicious data cannot enter the system
As a user, I want protection from XSS attacks so that my account remains secure
As a trainer, I want assurance that uploaded videos are legitimate so that I don't waste time on malicious files
As a platform operator, I want rate limiting on auth endpoints so that brute force attacks are prevented
acceptanceCriteria: All API routes validate inputs using Zod schemas and return 400 with clear error messages for invalid data
User-generated HTML content is sanitized before storage and display
File uploads validate type using magic numbers on server-side, reject non-video files
File size limit (500MB) enforced on server-side with clear error message
Rate limiting returns 429 status after threshold exceeded, with retry-after header
Authentication endpoints limited to 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP
CAPTCHA required on registration and after 3 failed login attempts

implementation.backend: Create /lib/validations.ts with Zod schemas for all data types (user, video, feedback, etc.). Create rate limiting middleware using @upstash/ratelimit with Redis. Add DOMPurify server-side package for HTML sanitization. Update all API routes to validate inputs before processing. Add file type validation using 'file-type' npm package to check magic numbers. Add CAPTCHA verification using Google reCAPTCHA v3 in auth routes.
implementation.frontend: Add Google reCAPTCHA script to layout. Update auth forms to include CAPTCHA token in requests. Display rate limit errors with countdown timer showing when user can retry. Add client-side validation messages that match server-side schemas for better UX.
implementation.database: No schema changes needed. May need to add RateLimitAttempt table to track failed attempts if not using Redis.

improvements.title: Add environment variable validation on startup
improvements.problem: The application can start with missing or invalid environment variables, leading to runtime crashes when features are accessed. AWS credentials and Stripe webhook secrets fallback to empty strings, which will cause silent failures in production. There's no validation that critical configuration is present before accepting requests.
improvements.solution: Create a startup validation module using Zod that validates all required environment variables before the Next.js app initializes. Define schemas for each environment variable type (string, URL, number, etc.). Fail fast with clear error messages indicating exactly which variables are missing or invalid. Include validation for format (e.g., URLs must be valid, secrets must be minimum length).
userStories: As a developer, I want the app to fail fast on startup with clear error messages so that I know exactly what configuration is missing
As a DevOps engineer, I want deployment to fail if environment variables are incorrect so that broken configurations don't reach production
As a platform operator, I want assurance that critical services (Stripe, AWS, email) are properly configured before the app starts accepting traffic
acceptanceCriteria: Application fails to start if any required environment variable is missing
Validation error messages clearly indicate which variables are missing or invalid
Environment variables are validated for correct format (URLs, email addresses, minimum secret length)
Type-safe access to environment variables throughout the codebase using validated schema
Documentation updated to list all required environment variables with descriptions
Development mode provides helpful suggestions for missing variables

implementation.backend: Create /lib/env.ts with Zod schema defining all environment variables with types and validation rules. Use z.string().min(), z.string().url(), z.string().email() as appropriate. Export validated env object. Import and execute validation in root layout.tsx and middleware.ts. Add try-catch to display formatted error with instructions.
implementation.frontend: No changes needed - validation happens server-side.
implementation.database: None

improvements.title: Implement actual video playback functionality
improvements.problem: This is a critical bug blocking core functionality. Videos are uploaded to S3 and stored in the database, but there's no way for users or trainers to actually watch them. The dashboard and feedback pages just display the S3 URL as text, making the entire platform unusable for its primary purpose. This is a fundamental feature gap that makes the app non-functional.
improvements.solution: Integrate a robust video player library (recommendation: react-player or video.js) that supports S3 presigned URLs, HLS streaming, and modern video formats. Implement player on both the user dashboard (to review their own videos) and trainer feedback page (to watch videos while providing feedback). Add playback controls: play/pause, timeline scrubbing, volume control, playback speed (0.5x, 1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, 2x), fullscreen mode. Add thumbnail preview on hover over timeline. Support keyboard shortcuts (space for play/pause, arrow keys for seeking).

As a trainer, I want to watch user videos with playback controls so that I can analyze form at different speeds and jump to specific moments
As a trainer, I want to pause at key frames so that I can provide detailed timestamped feedback
As a user, I want to watch videos in fullscreen mode so that I can see form details clearly

improvements.title: Add error boundaries to prevent app crashes
improvements.problem: The application has no error boundaries implemented, meaning any unhandled error in a React component will crash the entire application and show a blank white screen to users. This is especially problematic in production where errors may occur from network issues, API failures, or unexpected data formats. Users lose all context and cannot recover without a full page reload.
improvements.solution: Implement React error boundaries at strategic points in the component tree: (1) Root error boundary in root layout to catch all errors as last resort, (2) Page-level error boundaries for each major route, (3) Component-level boundaries for high-risk components (video player, file upload, payment forms). Display user-friendly error messages with recovery options (retry, go home, contact support). Log errors to monitoring service (future: Sentry) with component stack traces and user context.
userStories: As a user, I want graceful error handling so that one error doesn't crash the entire application
As a user, I want clear error messages so that I understand what went wrong and how to proceed
As a developer, I want error boundaries to catch errors so that I can log them for debugging without breaking UX
As a user, I want the ability to retry failed operations so that temporary issues don't require full page reloads
acceptanceCriteria: Root error boundary catches all uncaught errors and displays fallback UI
Page-level error boundaries prevent errors from affecting other pages
Error UI includes helpful message, error details (in dev mode), and action buttons
Retry button re-attempts the failed operation where applicable
'Go Home' button navigates to dashboard
Errors are logged with stack traces and user context for debugging
Error boundaries reset when user navigates to different page
Development mode shows detailed error info; production shows user-friendly messages

Wrap root layout in app/layout.tsx with root error boundary. Add error boundaries in app/dashboard/layout.tsx, app/upload/layout.tsx, app/trainers/layout.tsx. Add error boundaries around VideoPlayer and FileUpload components. Implement reset logic using key prop changes. Style error UI to match app design with error icon, message, and action buttons.

improvements.title: Fix Stripe webhook signature verification
improvements.problem: The Stripe webhook handler at /app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts uses 'process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET || ""' which means if the environment variable is missing, it defaults to an empty string. This will cause webhook signature verification to fail silently or pass with invalid signatures, leading to potential payment fraud, missed payment confirmations, and credit allocation failures. This is a critical security vulnerability that could result in financial loss.
improvements.solution: Remove the empty string fallback and make STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET a required environment variable that's validated on startup (ties into IMP-002). Update webhook handler to fail fast with proper error logging if secret is missing. Add comprehensive error handling for webhook processing failures with retry logic. Implement webhook event logging to database for audit trail and debugging. Add monitoring alerts for webhook failures.
userStories: As a platform operator, I want webhook signature verification to be secure so that only legitimate Stripe events are processed
As a user, I want my credit purchases to be reliably recorded so that I always receive the credits I paid for
As a developer, I want webhook failures to be logged and alerted so that payment issues are caught immediately
As a platform operator, I want an audit trail of all webhook events so that payment disputes can be investigated
acceptanceCriteria: STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is validated as required on app startup
Webhook handler returns 400 if signature verification fails
All webhook events are logged to database with status (success/failure)
Failed webhook processing triggers retry logic (up to 3 attempts)
Webhook failures send alerts to platform operators
Idempotency is enforced - duplicate events don't double-allocate credits
Test mode and production mode webhooks use separate secrets
Webhook processing uses database transactions to ensure consistency

implementation.backend: Update /lib/env.ts validation to include STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET as required. Update /app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts to remove || '' fallback. Add proper Stripe signature verification with try-catch and error logging. Create StripeWebhookLog model in Prisma schema to track all events. Implement transaction wrapper for credit allocation. Add idempotency check using Stripe event ID. Create /lib/stripe/webhooks.ts with handler functions for each event type (checkout.session.completed, etc.). Add retry queue for failed events.
implementation.frontend: None - webhooks are server-side only.
implementation.database: Add StripeWebhookLog model: id, eventId (unique), eventType, status, payload (json), error, attempts, createdAt, processedAt. Add index on eventId for idempotency checks.

improvements.title: Add pagination and infinite scroll to list pages
improvements.problem: All list views (videos, feedback, trainers) use simple 'take' limits without pagination, which will cause severe performance and UX issues at scale. The dashboard limits to 10 videos with no way to see older uploads. As users accumulate hundreds of videos or trainers register by the hundreds, queries will become slow and users can't access all data. Database queries fetch all related data without pagination, creating N+1 query problems.
improvements.solution: Implement cursor-based pagination for all list endpoints using Prisma's cursor pagination. On the frontend, implement infinite scroll using React Query's infinite queries with intersection observer. For trainers page, implement traditional page-based pagination with page numbers. Add 'Load More' buttons as fallback for users who prefer explicit loading. Optimize queries to fetch only necessary fields and implement proper indexing.
userStories: As a user with many videos, I want to scroll through all my uploads so that I can access my entire history
As a trainer, I want to browse all available review requests so that I can choose which ones to accept
As a user, I want pages to load quickly even with hundreds of videos in the system so that the app feels responsive
As a platform with thousands of users, I want efficient database queries so that server costs remain manageable

Return { videos, nextCursor }. Apply same pattern to /app/api/feedback/route.ts and /app/api/trainers/route.ts. Add database indexes on createdAt for videos and feedback, on rating for trainers. Optimize queries using select() to fetch only displayed fields.

Implement intersection observer on sentinel element at list bottom to trigger fetchNextPage. Add 'Loading more...' indicator. Create reusable /components/InfiniteList.tsx component. For trainers page, implement page-based pagination using searchParams. Add PageNavigation component with prev/next buttons and page numbers. Update list components to handle loading and hasNextPage states.

improvements.title: Implement email verification and password reset
improvements.problem: Users can register with any email address without verification, leading to fake accounts, typos preventing access, and spam registrations. There's no password reset functionality, so users who forget passwords are permanently locked out and must create new accounts, losing all their data. These are essential security and UX features that should never ship to production without implementation.
improvements.solution: Implement email verification flow: after registration, send verification email with token link. Users must verify email before uploading videos (can browse, but upload is blocked). Add password reset flow: user requests reset, receives email with time-limited token, creates new password. Store verification and reset tokens in database with expiration. Add UI for resending verification emails and displaying verification status.

As a user who forgot my password, I want to reset it via email so that I can regain access to my account
As a platform operator, I want email verification to prevent spam accounts and ensure we can contact users
As a user with a typo in my email, I want clear error messages so that I can re-register with the correct email

improvements.title: Fix Trainer relation queries in feedback routes
improvements.problem: In /app/api/feedback/route.ts lines 28-31, the code attempts to access 'name' and 'role' fields directly on the Trainer model, but these fields don't exist there - they're on the related User model. This will cause runtime errors whenever feedback is fetched. The query is: trainer: { select: { name: true, role: true } } but should be trainer: { select: { user: { select: { name: true } } } }. This is a critical bug that breaks the feedback listing functionality.
improvements.solution: Fix the Prisma query to properly access nested User fields through the Trainer relation. Update all instances where Trainer data is fetched to use correct relation path. Add type checking to ensure queries match schema. Test all affected endpoints to ensure data is returned correctly.

As a developer, I want type-safe database queries so that schema mismatches are caught at compile time
As a trainer, I want my name to display correctly on feedback so that users know who provided the review

improvements.title: Optimize database queries and add caching
improvements.problem: Database queries fetch entire objects instead of selecting specific fields, causing over-fetching. Trainer listings and user credits are queried on every page load without caching. No Redis or in-memory caching layer exists. Prisma queries don't use select() optimization. This results in unnecessary database load, slower response times, and higher hosting costs. As the platform scales, these inefficiencies will compound.
improvements.solution: Implement Redis caching for frequently accessed, rarely changing data (trainer listings, user profile info). Optimize all Prisma queries to use select() and only fetch needed fields. Add database indexes on commonly filtered columns (createdAt on videos/feedback, rating on trainers). Implement cache invalidation strategies. Add query result caching with TTL. Use Prisma's query optimization features.
userStories: As a user, I want pages to load quickly so that I have a smooth browsing experience
As a platform operator, I want optimized queries so that we can handle more users without expensive database upgrades
As a developer, I want caching infrastructure so that frequently accessed data doesn't hit the database unnecessarily
As a platform at scale, I want efficient resource usage so that hosting costs stay manageable
acceptanceCriteria: Trainer listings are cached in Redis with 5-minute TTL
User profile data cached per session with smart invalidation on updates
All Prisma queries use select() to fetch only required fields

Cache invalidation works correctly when data is updated
Database indexes created for: (userId, createdAt) on videos, (videoId, status) on feedback, (rating) on trainers

Update /app/api/trainers/route.ts to check cache before querying DB. Update all queries to use select(): .findMany({ select: { id: true, title: true, createdAt: true } }). Remove unnecessary includes that fetch related data not displayed. Add database indexes via Prisma migration. Implement cache invalidation in mutation endpoints (when trainer updates profile, invalidate 'trainers:list'). Add caching middleware for API routes. Set appropriate TTLs: short (1-5 min) for frequently changing data, long (1 hour) for static data.

improvements.title: Implement proper file cleanup for abandoned uploads
improvements.problem: When users request a presigned URL for video upload but never complete the upload or never create the Video database record, the file remains in S3 forever. Over time, this accumulates storage costs for abandoned partial uploads. There's no cleanup job to identify and remove these orphaned files. This is a slow leak that will increase AWS costs over time.
improvements.solution: Implement cleanup system: (1) Track all presigned URLs generated with expiration timestamps, (2) Create scheduled job (cron or serverless function) that runs daily/weekly, (3) Identify S3 files that have no corresponding Video record and are older than 48 hours, (4) Delete orphaned files from S3, (5) Log cleanup actions for audit trail. Consider implementing lifecycle rules in S3 for automatic cleanup of old files in specific prefixes.
userStories: As a platform operator, I want orphaned files cleaned up automatically so that storage costs don't balloon unnecessarily
As a developer, I want visibility into cleanup operations so that I can verify it's working correctly
As a finance manager, I want optimized S3 usage so that we minimize cloud infrastructure costs

Failures are logged and alerted to developers

S3 lifecycle rules configured as backup cleanup mechanism

Implement S3 lifecycle rule: transition files in temp-uploads/ prefix to deletion after 7 days. Create /app/api/admin/cleanup/route.ts POST for manual triggering.

improvements.problem: Videos are stored in original upload format without processing, leading to large file sizes, slow playback, compatibility issues, and high bandwidth costs. improvements.solution: Implement server-side video processing pipeline using FFmpeg or cloud service (Mux, Cloudinary, Cloudflare Stream): (1) Generate thumbnail at 2-second mark, (2) Compress video to web-optimized format (H.264/AAC), (3) Create multiple quality versions (360p, 720p, 1080p), (4) Support adaptive bitrate streaming (HLS), (5) Store processed versions in S3, (6) Update Video model with thumbnail URL and quality URLs.

improvements.title: Add comprehensive security headers and CSP
improvements.problem: The application lacks security headers, leaving it vulnerable to clickjacking, MIME sniffing attacks, XSS, and other common web vulnerabilities. No Content Security Policy is configured, meaning malicious scripts could be injected and executed. Missing Strict-Transport-Security header allows downgrade attacks. These are basic security hardening steps that should be implemented before production launch.
improvements.solution: Implement comprehensive security headers using Next.js configuration: (1) Content-Security-Policy restricting script sources, (2) X-Frame-Options to prevent clickjacking, (3) X-Content-Type-Options to prevent MIME sniffing, (4) Strict-Transport-Security for HTTPS enforcement, (5) Referrer-Policy to control referrer information, (6) Permissions-Policy to disable unnecessary browser features. Test CSP in report-only mode first to avoid breaking functionality.
userStories: As a user, I want protection from XSS attacks so that malicious scripts cannot steal my data
As a user, I want the platform to be secure so that my personal information is protected
As a platform operator, I want to follow security best practices so that we pass security audits and maintain user trust
As a developer, I want CSP violations reported so that I can identify and fix security issues
acceptanceCriteria: Content-Security-Policy header configured with appropriate directives for all resources
CSP allows app functionality (scripts, styles, images, fonts, media) while blocking unauthorized sources
X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN to prevent clickjacking
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff to prevent MIME sniffing
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
Permissions-Policy disables unnecessary features (geolocation, microphone, camera)
Security headers present on all routes including API routes
CSP violations logged and monitored
Security score of A or A+ on securityheaders.com
No browser console CSP violation errors for normal usage

implementation.backend: Update next.config.js to add headers configuration. Define CSP policy allowing scripts from 'self', styles from 'self' and 'unsafe-inline' (for Tailwind), images from 'self' and S3 domain, media from S3 domain, connect to API routes and Stripe. Start with CSP in report-only mode: Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only. Set up CSP violation reporting endpoint /app/api/csp-violations/route.ts to log violations. After testing, switch to enforcing mode. Add security headers to middleware.ts for API routes. Configure HSTS with long max-age. Add Permissions-Policy header disabling unused features.
implementation.frontend: Remove any inline scripts (move to script files). Ensure all external resources are allowlisted in CSP. Add nonce or hash for any necessary inline scripts. Test all functionality with CSP enforced. Update script/link tags with appropriate attributes.
implementation.database: Optional: Create CspViolation model to log CSP violations: id, documentUri, violatedDirective, blockedUri, sourceFile, lineNumber, userAgent, createdAt. Useful for monitoring security issues.

improvements.title: Optimize bundle size and implement code splitting
improvements.problem: The application ships a large JavaScript bundle to all users without code splitting beyond Next.js defaults. Heavy libraries (video player, file upload, Stripe) are loaded even on pages that don't use them. No bundle size analysis has been done. This results in slower initial page loads, especially on mobile devices or slow connections, negatively impacting user experience and SEO rankings.
improvements.solution: Analyze current bundle size, identify heavy dependencies, implement lazy loading for heavy components, add route-based code splitting, remove unused dependencies, set up bundle size budgets in CI to prevent regressions. Use next/dynamic for component-level code splitting. Optimize third-party scripts to load asynchronously.
userStories: As a user on slow connection, I want the app to load quickly so that I can start using it without long waits
As a developer, I want visibility into bundle size so that I can make informed decisions about dependencies
As a platform operator, I want optimized performance so that SEO rankings improve and users don't bounce
As a developer, I want bundle size budgets so that new features don't accidentally bloat the app
acceptanceCriteria: Bundle analysis report generated showing size breakdown by chunk
Main bundle size reduced by at least 30% through code splitting and optimization
Video player component lazy loaded only on pages with videos
File upload component (react-dropzone) lazy loaded only on upload page
Stripe components lazy loaded only on checkout/payment pages
Initial JavaScript payload <200KB gzipped for main page
Lighthouse performance score >90 for landing page
Time to Interactive (TTI) <4 seconds on 3G connection
CI pipeline checks bundle size and fails if budget exceeded
Unused dependencies removed from package.json

implementation.backend: No changes needed - optimization is frontend only.
implementation.frontend: Install @next/bundle-analyzer, configure in next.config.js. Run 'npm run analyze' to generate bundle report. Identify heavy chunks (likely react-player, @aws-sdk, stripe). Update heavy components to use dynamic imports: const VideoPlayer = dynamic(() => import('./VideoPlayer'), { loading: () => <Skeleton /> }). Lazy load video player, file upload, chart libraries. Use next/script with strategy='lazyOnload' for non-critical third-party scripts. Audit dependencies with 'npm ls' and remove unused packages. Configure webpack to optimize chunk splitting. Add bundle size budget in package.json using bundlesize package. Update CI workflow to run bundlesize check. Implement route-based prefetching only for likely next pages (don't prefetch all routes).

improvements.title: Add trainer availability and scheduling system
improvements.problem: Trainers have no way to set their availability or maximum workload, leading to potential burnout when assigned too many videos. improvements.title: Implement autosave for feedback composition
improvements.problem: Trainers spend significant time writing detailed feedback and timestamped comments. If they accidentally navigate away, close the tab, or experience a browser crash, all their work is lost. This is extremely frustrating and wastes billable time. No autosave or draft system exists. This is a common pain point that should be addressed to improve trainer satisfaction and retention.
improvements.solution: Implement autosave functionality for feedback forms: (1) Auto-save draft to localStorage every 30 seconds as trainer types, (2) On page load, check for saved draft and offer to restore, (3) Visual indicator showing 'Saving...', 'Saved', or 'Unsaved changes', (4) Optional: Save drafts to database for cross-device access, (5) Clear draft after successful submission.
userStories: As a trainer, I want my feedback auto-saved so that I don't lose work if I accidentally navigate away
As a trainer, I want to see save status so that I know my work is protected
As a trainer, I want draft recovery so that I can resume work after browser crash
As a trainer writing long feedback, I want peace of mind so that I can take breaks without losing progress

Manual 'Save draft' button for trainers who want to explicitly save

implementation.database: Optional: FeedbackDraft model: id, trainerId (Int, relation), videoId (Int, relation), content (Json), lastSaved (DateTime @default(now())), @@unique([trainerId, videoId]).

improvements.title: Improve accessibility to WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
improvements.problem: The application lacks accessibility features, making it difficult or impossible for users with disabilities to use. No ARIA labels, keyboard navigation not verified, color contrast not checked, no screen reader testing. This excludes a significant portion of potential users (15-20% of population has some disability) and may violate legal requirements (ADA, Section 508). Poor accessibility also hurts SEO.
improvements.solution: Conduct accessibility audit and implement WCAG 2.1 AA compliance: (1) Add proper ARIA labels to all interactive elements, (2) Ensure full keyboard navigation support, (3) Verify color contrast ratios meet WCAG standards, (4) Test with screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), (5) Implement focus management for modals and dialogs, (6) Add skip-to-content link, (7) Support reduced motion preferences, (8) Add alt text strategy for images, (9) Ensure form errors are announced to screen readers.
userStories: As a keyboard user, I want to navigate the entire app without a mouse so that I can use the platform effectively
As a screen reader user, I want proper ARIA labels so that I understand the purpose of each element
As a user with low vision, I want sufficient color contrast so that text is readable
As a user with motion sensitivity, I want reduced motion support so that animations don't cause discomfort
As a user with disabilities, I want an accessible platform so that I can improve my fitness like anyone else
acceptanceCriteria: All interactive elements have appropriate ARIA labels (aria-label, aria-labelledby)
Full keyboard navigation works: Tab through all interactive elements in logical order
Focus visible on all interactive elements with clear visual indicator
Skip-to-content link appears on Tab from top of page
All form inputs have associated labels (htmlFor matching input id)
Form errors announced to screen readers (aria-live, role='alert')
Color contrast ratio ≥4.5:1 for normal text, ≥3:1 for large text
Modals trap focus and return to trigger element on close
Reduced motion preference supported (prefers-reduced-motion media query)
Alt text provided for all informative images
Video player has keyboard controls and supports captions (future)
Automated accessibility tests pass (axe-core, Pa11y)
Manual screen reader testing completed on 3 major flows
Lighthouse accessibility score ≥90

Add skip link: Skip to content with CSS to show on focus. Implement focus trapping in modals using focus-trap-react or Radix UI Dialog. Add aria-live regions for notifications and errors. Update color palette to meet contrast ratios - use WebAIM contrast checker. Add prefers-reduced-motion media queries to disable/reduce animations. Create accessibility testing checklist for new features. Add keyboard event handlers (onKeyDown) for custom interactive components. Ensure focus moves logically with Tab key. Add visually hidden text for icon-only buttons. Test with NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver and document issues. Fix focus styles - make sure outline or ring is visible on all interactive elements.
